title: Molecular design beyond training data with novel extended objective functionals of generative AI models driven by quantum annealing computer
excerpt: This study presents a novel approach to molecular design that extends generative AI model capabilities beyond their training data through the use of extended objective functionals combined with quantum annealing computing. The method integrates quantum annealing technology to optimize molecular structure generation, enabling the discovery of compounds with properties not fully represented in the original training datasets. This approach bridges computational chemistry and quantum computing to enhance the exploration of chemical space for drug discovery applications.

title: Proteome-scale quantification of the interactions driving condensate formation of intrinsically disordered proteins
excerpt: This study quantifies protein-protein interactions at the proteome scale that drive condensate formation by intrinsically disordered proteins, providing systematic characterization of the molecular mechanisms underlying phase separation. The research integrates experimental and computational approaches to identify and measure the specific interactions responsible for condensate assembly across multiple proteins.

title: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) in neuropsychiatric drug discovery: current challenges and future directions
excerpt: This review examines the application of artificial intelligence in neuropsychiatric drug discovery, identifying current technical and practical obstacles while proposing strategies to advance the field. The authors discuss how AI can address existing limitations in the drug development process for psychiatric medications, though significant challenges remain in implementation and validation. Key considerations for regulatory and development professionals include data quality requirements, model interpretability, and the integration of AI tools within established drug discovery workflows.

title: Circulating inflammatory, redox, and apoptosis-related alterations in drug-naive idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: an exploratory case-control study
excerpt: This exploratory case-control study examined circulating biomarkers related to inflammation, oxidative stress, and apoptosis in treatment-naive patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis compared to controls. The research identified specific alterations in these three pathological pathways that may contribute to disease pathogenesis in IPF. These findings provide preliminary evidence of the molecular mechanisms underlying idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis that could inform future therapeutic development.

This integrated approach supports structure-aware drug discovery by generating candidate ligands and predicting their binding characteristics simultaneously.

title: Ferrocene-based Drug Molecules in Medicinal Chemistry: Comprehensive Insights into their Chemistry, Pharmacology, and Emerging Therapeutic Applications
excerpt: Ferrocene is an organometallic compound discovered in 1951 that continues to be of interest in medicinal chemistry due to its exceptional stability and has emerging applications as a basis for drug molecules. This comprehensive review examines the chemistry, pharmacology, and therapeutic potential of ferrocene-based drug molecules in medicinal chemistry.

title: Gabapentin Extended Release Tablets in Healthy Subjects Under Fed Conditions: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Treatment, Two-Period, Two-Sequence, Single Dose, Crossover, Comparative Bioavailability Study
excerpt: This randomized, open-label, crossover bioavailability study compared gabapentin extended-release tablets in healthy subjects under fed conditions using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence design with single-dose administration. The study was conducted by Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited and collaborating institutions to establish comparative bioavailability data for the formulation. Published in Drugs R&D, the research provides pharmacokinetic characterization of gabapentin extended-release tablets relevant to regulatory assessment and formulation development.

title: Cytotoxicity and synergistic anticancer activity of Kigelia africana (Lam.) Benth. and Spathodea campanulata P. Beauv. polyherbal extracts
excerpt: This study evaluated the cytotoxic and anticancer properties of polyherbal extracts combining Kigelia africana and Spathodea campanulata, investigating their individual and synergistic effects against cancer cell lines. The research demonstrates that these plant extracts exhibit cytotoxic activity and produce enhanced anticancer effects when used in combination. Findings suggest potential therapeutic applications of these polyherbal formulations in cancer treatment.
